nativeRange.habitat: The Tassle Filefish is found in weedy areas of coastal reefs and trawling grounds across the Indo-West Pacific, generally at depths of 2 to 25 m. Its tasselled, mottled body and dark, winding stripe pattern are widely regarded as providing effective camouflage among macroalgae and seagrass beds.

diet.notes: Has been documented as an omnivore that grazes on macroalgae and microalgae and feeds on benthic invertebrates including small crustaceans, tubeworms and polychaetes, and occasionally polyps of soft and large-polyp stony corals. In captivity, varied feedings of mysis, krill, chopped shrimp/squid/clam, nori and spirulina-based foods are generally recommended; multiple small feedings per day (three or more) tend to work well, especially during acclimation.

goodWith: peaceful fish of similar or larger size
avoidWith: shrimps, crabs and other small crustaceans (may be eaten)
LPS and soft corals (may nip polyps)
boisterous or fast-feeding species that outcompete for food

summary: A peaceful saltwater filefish growing to around 30 cm, distinctive for its tasselled, algae-like camouflage. Generally recommended for large fish-only or FOWLR systems as it has been reported to nip corals and consume small invertebrates.
careNotes:
The Tassle Filefish (Chaetodermis penicilligerus), also sold under the names Tasseled Filefish, Prickly Leatherjacket, and Leafy Filefish, is generally considered peaceful toward unrelated tankmates but is not regarded as reliably reef-safe — individuals have been reported to nip at LPS (large-polyp stony) and soft corals and to consume tubeworms, shrimps, crabs, and snails. It is best suited to fish-only or fish-only-with-live-rock (FOWLR) systems.

One of the species' most striking features is its tasselled, weed-like skin filaments and winding dark stripe pattern over a pale or greenish body, which provide camouflage among macroalgae and seagrass in its native coastal-reef habitat. Aquariums are generally advised to include weedy or algal areas, rockwork with shelters, and open swimming space to reflect these conditions.

This species tends to be shy and skittish when first introduced and is commonly considered slow to acclimate. Frequent, small feedings offered several times per day are often recommended while it settles in, and a varied diet of mysis shrimp, krill, chopped seafood, nori, and vitamin-enriched preparations tends to work well long-term.

Conspecific aggression has been reported toward other filefish and related species in the order Tetraodontiformes, so the Tassle Filefish is generally best kept as a single specimen unless the system is very large.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Collection page used as primary; LiveAquaria's individual species page (https://www.liveaquaria.com/products/tassle-filefish) also fetched separately and provided care level (easy), temperament (peaceful), reef compatibility (no), water conditions (72-78°F, pH 8.1-8.4, SG 1.020-1.025, dKH 8-12), max size (~12 in / 30.5 cm), minimum tank size (180 gal / ~680 L), and diet (omnivore; meaty foods + frozen algae, fed 3+ times daily).

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Primary cross-check. Confirmed scientific classification (Monacanthidae, Tetraodontiformes), max size 31 cm TL, Indo-West Pacific distribution (Indo-Malayan region to Australia, north to Japan), wild temperature range 24.1-29.3°C (mean 28.6°C), depth 2-25 m, reef-associated weedy/coastal habitat, omnivorous trophic level 2.8, IUCN Least Concern (2017).

additional.name: ReefApp Encyclopedia
additional.notes: Secondary cross-check. Provided minimum tank size 700 L, salinity SG ~1.026, temperature ~26°C, adult size 31 cm, diet (macroalgae, microalgae, small crustaceans, occasional LPS/soft coral polyps), temperament peaceful but possibly aggressive to conspecifics, reef compatibility 'with luck', care level moderate, feeding several times daily especially when newly added.

additional.name: reefs.com Tassle Filefish profile
additional.notes: Additional cross-check via web search aggregation. Confirmed 180 gal minimum tank, 12 in max size, temperament peaceful, reef compatibility no, conspecific aggression toward other filefish, diet of meaty foods plus frozen algae fed 3+ times daily. Direct page fetch returned 403; data taken from web-search summary.
